docs: https://developer.breezy.hr/ ; full 46-endpoint surface extracted from the live ReadMe-hosted OpenAPI 3.1 spec embedded in the reference page markup (https://developer.breezy.hr/reference/company-positions and sibling pages), not just prose summaries
scope: Pass B full-surface expansion: every documented Breezy v3 endpoint is enumerated below (46 distinct operations; the ReadMe reference page for /signin also renders a stray duplicate '(COPY)' entry that is not a real second endpoint and is excluded from this count). 9 read streams now covered (positions, pipelines, candidates — all 3 retained at wave2 capability parity — plus departments, categories, custom_attributes_candidate, custom_attributes_position, questionnaires, templates) and 6 write actions (create_position, update_position, update_position_state, update_candidate, move_candidate_stage, create_candidate). The root-level /companies and /company/{company_id} endpoints are a genuine ENGINE_GAP (this bundle's base.url is company-scoped for every other stream, and the dialect has no per-stream base-URL-override mechanism that survives InterpolatePath's urlencode-by-default without corrupting the scheme — see docs.md Known limits). Excluded: the entire per-candidate sub-resource detail surface (assessments/background-checks/conversation/custom-attribute/custom-fields/documents/education/meta/questionnaire responses/resume/scorecard/send-questionnaire/stream/work-history — each requires an already-known candidate_id with no incremental value beyond the candidates stream's own record, and several are write-only or return binary/file content), position-scoped custom-fields and team-member sub-resources (same already-known-id-required shape), the session-based /signin+/signout+password-reset auth flow (this connector uses a pre-issued raw API key via the Authorization header, not Breezy's email/password session-token flow — a fundamentally different credential model), and single-user profile introspection endpoints.

excluded.reason: single-position detail; identical record shape to the positions list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream

position-scoped custom field VALUES require an already-known position_id and duplicate metadata the custom_attributes_position stream (the field DEFINITIONS) already covers; not a distinct list-shaped resource worth a dedicated fan_out this pass
single custom-field-value mutation requiring an already-known custom_field_id with no discovery stream feeding it in this pass
position-level activity/comment stream requiring an already-known position_id; a write-heavy collaboration feed, not a syncable data object
position-scoped hiring-team-member list requiring an already-known position_id; out of scope for this pass's breadth-vs-cost triage
candidate creation via multipart resume file upload, not a plain-JSON body mutation this dialect's write model fits; create_candidate already covers structured candidate creation
single-candidate detail; identical record shape to the candidates list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
requires an already-known candidate_id with no incremental sync value beyond the candidates stream's own record; a third-party assessment-integration result sub-resource, not core hiring data
requires an already-known candidate_id; a sensitive third-party background-check result sub-resource, out of scope for this pass's breadth-vs-cost triage
requires an already-known candidate_id; an email-thread sub-resource, not a top-level syncable object
sends a real email to the candidate on the caller's behalf; a communication action, not a data mutation, and requires an already-known candidate_id
requires an already-known candidate_id and custom-attribute id with no discovery stream feeding it in this pass
requires an already-known candidate_id; duplicates metadata the custom_attributes_candidate stream (the field DEFINITIONS) already covers
requires an already-known candidate_id and custom_field_id with no discovery stream feeding it in this pass
requires an already-known candidate_id; returns file metadata/binary attachment references, not a plain data-sync object
multipart file upload attaching a document to a candidate, not a plain-JSON body mutation this dialect's write model fits
requires an already-known candidate_id; a candidate sub-object array replace, out of scope for this pass's breadth-vs-cost triage (update_candidate already covers the core candidate profile fields)
requires an already-known candidate_id; an aggregate 'everything about this candidate in one call' convenience endpoint whose constituent parts are each already excluded above for the same reason

moves a candidate to a DIFFERENT position AND stage in one call; move_candidate_stage (PUT .../stage) already covers the same-position stage-transition mutation this dialect's write model fits, and cross-position moves are a rarer compound operation out of scope this pass
submits questionnaire response answers on the candidate's behalf; requires an already-known candidate_questionnaire_id with no discovery stream feeding it in this pass
requires an already-known candidate_id; a candidate-scoped questionnaire-response sub-resource, out of scope for this pass's breadth-vs-cost triage
multipart resume file upload/replace for an existing candidate, not a plain-JSON body mutation
downloads the candidate's resume file content, not a JSON record
requires an already-known candidate_id and interview-scorecard structure; a specialized interview-feedback sub-resource, out of scope for this pass's breadth-vs-cost triage
sends a real email/questionnaire invitation to the candidate on the caller's behalf; a communication action requiring an already-known candidate_id and questionnaire_id
candidate-level activity/comment stream requiring an already-known candidate_id; a write-heavy collaboration feed, not a syncable data object
posts an internal note/comment to the candidate's activity stream on the caller's behalf; a collaboration action, not a data mutation, and requires an already-known candidate_id
requires an already-known candidate_id; a candidate sub-object array replace, out of scope for this pass's breadth-vs-cost triage (update_candidate already covers the core candidate profile fields)
single-pipeline detail; identical record shape to the pipelines list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream

ENGINE_GAP: this is a ROOT-level endpoint (no /company/{company_id} prefix at all), but this bundle's base.url is company-scoped ("{{ config.base_url }}/company/{{ secrets.company_id }}") for every other stream; the dialect's InterpolatePath applies urlencode-by-default to every resolved {{ }} reference, so a stream path templating {{ config.base_url }} directly (to reach back above the company-scoped base) would corrupt the scheme/host into a percent-encoded string, and a hardcoded absolute literal would silently ignore a caller-configured base_url override — a real accepted-input divergence, not a defensible workaround. See docs.md Known limits.

same ENGINE_GAP as GET /companies: reachable in principle (this bundle already scopes every other request to one company_id), but a dedicated 'read this one company's own metadata' stream would need to select {{ secrets.company_id }} as the record's own id with no list envelope at all (records.path: "." over a single object works, per conventions.md's connsdk.RecordsAt single-object tolerance) — scoped out this pass since it returns exactly one static record identical to the config the caller already supplied, no new information; not the same base-URL escape problem as the plural /companies endpoint
an on-demand lookup-by-email_address query, not an enumerable list stream (the caller must already know the email to search for); the candidates stream already syncs every candidate on every position
single-questionnaire detail; identical record shape to the questionnaires list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
single-template detail; identical record shape to the templates list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream

email/password session-token sign-in — this connector authenticates with a pre-issued raw API key via the Authorization header (spec.json's api_key), a fundamentally different credential model than Breezy's session-token flow; the two are not composable within one auth block
invalidates a session access token obtained via the excluded /signin flow; not applicable to this connector's raw-API-key auth model

resets the AUTHENTICATED DASHBOARD USER's own login password — a human-account-security action tied to the session-token auth flow this connector does not use, not a data mutation on any Breezy HR business object
authenticated-user profile introspection (or lookup by user_id), not a syncable business-data object stream
authenticated-user's own extended profile/company-context introspection, not a syncable business-data object stream
